title: External Integrations, Distributed Effects, and Resilience · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for external integrations, distributed effects, and resilience contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in controlled integration and resilience evidence and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.
Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.
Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Integration Authority
steps.instruction:
debug case — A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

Inspect the Integration Authority trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Integration Authority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Integration Authority, keep Integration Authority connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Integration Authority, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Integration Authority, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Integration Authority precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Integration Authority, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Integration Authority, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An API key and SDK establish permission to use a service.

stimulus.title: Integration Authority case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An API key and SDK establish permission to use a service.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries. Artifact under review: controlled integration and resilience evidence.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Integration Authority, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Vendor Exit
steps.instruction:
Append a capstone-vendor-exit-lm5ihu: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Vendor Exit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Vendor Exit into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Vendor Exit, keep Vendor Exit connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
capstone-vendor-exit-lm5ihu:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Vendor Exit, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Vendor Exit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Vendor Exit, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Vendor Exit, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An interface abstraction makes vendor exit inexpensive.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Vendor Exit
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Vendor Exit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment.
